original_image_tags: A single page of a typed memorandum, viewed from the reverse side (text is mirrored/bleed-through). The page has two hole-punch marks at the top. At the bottom, there are two stamps. The left stamp reads "RECEIVED-LAAD FBI U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE" and is dated "DEC 30 2 48 PM '48". The right stamp is partially visible, reading "RECEIVED FBI CHICAGO FIELD OFFICE" and is dated "DEC 31 C 24 AM '48". There is handwritten text in the bottom left margin.
Stamped in blue ink at bottom left.
Stamped date and time within the left stamp.
Stamped in blue ink at bottom right.
Stamped date and time within the right stamp.

elements.description: Page consists entirely of faded, mirrored typed text from a carbon copy or reverse bleed-through of a US government memorandum, including several stamped annotations at the bottom such as 'RECEIVED-LADD', 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE FBI', 'DEC 30 2 48 PM '48', and 'RETURNED'. There are also two hole-punch marks at the top and a small handwritten squiggle at the bottom left.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the presence of two hole-punch marks, two stamps at the bottom, and specific quoted text (e.g., 'RECEIVED-LAAD/LADD', 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE', 'DEC 30 2 48 PM '48'), but they differ on some details (one mentions 'RECEIVED FBI CHICAGO FIELD OFFICE' and 'RETURNED' while the other does not, and one mentions a handwritten squiggle while the other mentions handwritten text in the margin) without a direct contradiction on quoted text.
